$comment: Actio macro layer. This is the ONLY hand-authored schema surface. `scripts/build-schema.mjs` merges it onto the vendored official GitHub Actions workflow schema (vendor/github-workflow.json) to produce actio.schema.json. Edit macros here, then run `npm run schema:build -w actio-core`.

meta.title: Actio workflow (.actio.yml)
meta.description: Source language for the Actio transpiler: the official GitHub Actions workflow schema (inherited wholesale from SchemaStore) plus Actio macro keywords (fragments/inject, retry, dynamic-matrix, fallback, for-each) that compile into standard workflow YAML. Every standard GitHub Actions key, event, and value is validated and autocompleted from the upstream schema; the macro keys are additive. See https://github.com/austenstone/actio.

fragments.title: fragments (Actio macro)
fragments.description: Actio macro. Reusable, named lists of steps. Splice a fragment into a job with `- inject: <name>`. The `fragments` key is stripped from the generated workflow.
fragments.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: reusable, named step lists.

Define ordered step lists once, then splice them into any job with `- inject: <name>`. The `fragments` key is stripped from the generated workflow.

```yaml
fragments:
  setup:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
jobs:
  build:
    steps:
      - inject: setup
      - run: npm ci
```

additionalProperties.description: An ordered list of steps to inject wherever this fragment is referenced.

params.description: Actio macro. Typed compile-time parameters. References are resolved with `{{ params.<name> }}` or `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` and stripped from emitted workflow YAML.

let.title: let (Actio macro)
let.description: Actio macro. File-local compile-time constants. Each value is a literal or a whole-value `{{ expression }}` evaluated at build time; reference results with `{{ let.<name> }}`. Constants may depend on earlier `params`/`let` entries. The `let` key is stripped from the generated workflow.
let.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: file-local compile-time constants.

Each entry is a literal or a single whole-value `{{ expression }}` evaluated by the compile-time engine; the typed result is referenced elsewhere with `{{ let.<name> }}`. Entries may reference earlier `params`/`let` values. Only compile-time expressions are allowed: a `${{ }}` runtime expression or a runtime context (e.g. `github.*`) is rejected. The `let` key is stripped from the generated workflow.

```yaml
params:
  base: { type: number, default: 2 }
let:
  doubled: "{{ params.base * 2 }}"
  label: "build-{{ let.doubled }}"
```

_anchors.title: _anchors (Actio macro)
_anchors.description: Actio macro. Reserved top-level home for YAML anchor definitions (`&name`) referenced elsewhere with `*name`. A `- *alias` of a sequence is flattened into the surrounding steps list at compile time. The `_anchors` key is stripped from the generated workflow.
_anchors.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: a parking spot for YAML anchors.

Native YAML anchors (`&`/`*`/`<<:`) already own no-arg reuse; `_anchors:` just gives anchor libraries a reserved home so they do not pollute real workflow keys. A `- *alias` whose anchor is a step list is flattened into the surrounding steps. The `_anchors` key is stripped from the generated workflow.

```yaml
_anchors:
  setup: &setup
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - *setup
      - run: npm ci
```

templates.title: templates (Actio macro)
templates.description: Actio macro. Parameterized, named step lists. Splice a template into a job with `- inject: <name>` and pass typed `with:` args resolved at compile time via `{{ args.<name> }}`. The `templates` key is stripped from the generated workflow.
templates.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: parameterized step lists.

Like `fragments:`, but with typed `params:`. Inject with `- inject: <name>` and a `with:` mapping; `{{ args.<name> }}` references in the body are resolved and erased at compile time. The `templates` key is stripped from the generated workflow.

```yaml
templates:
  greet:
    params:
      who: { type: string }
    steps:
      - run: echo "hello {{ args.who }}"
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - inject: greet
        with: { who: world }
```

job-defaults.description: Actio macro. Default job-level settings merged into jobs at compile time. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

executors.description: Actio macro. Named runner/container/service presets referenced by `jobs.<id>.executor`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

call-templates.description: Actio macro (compile-time). Named reusable-workflow call-job templates referenced by `jobs.<id>.extends`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

injection-hoist.description: Actio macro. Global default injection-hoist mode for every `run:` in the workflow. Overridden by job- and step-level `injection-hoist`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

coercion.description: Actio macro. YAML type-coercion guard mode: `fix` (single-quote emitted string scalars that a YAML 1.1 consumer would mis-type, e.g. `no`/`on`/`1:30`/`2024-01-01`, default), `warn` (diagnose but leave unquoted), or `off`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

finally.title: finally (Actio macro)
finally.description: Actio macro. Workflow-scoped teardown. Each named entry compiles to an aggregator job that `needs:` every real job and runs on the normal path (`if: !cancelled()`), plus an auto cancel-companion (`if: cancelled()`, `timeout-minutes: 5`). Nest `on-success`/`on-failure`/`on-abort` for outcome-branch jobs. Use `ensure:` for job/step-scoped teardown, not `finally:`.
finally.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: workflow-scoped teardown (sibling to `jobs:`).

Each named entry compiles to an aggregator job that `needs:` every real job and runs on the normal path (`if: !cancelled()`), plus an auto cancel-companion (`if: cancelled()`, `timeout-minutes: 5`). Nest `on-success`/`on-failure`/`on-abort` for outcome-branch jobs; `on-abort: []` opts out of the cancel job.

```yaml
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./deploy.sh
finally:
  teardown:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./cleanup.sh
```

For job/step teardown use `ensure:` instead.

reusable.title: reusable (Actio macro)
reusable.description: Actio macro. Declare a workflow as both callable (`workflow_call`) and dispatchable (`workflow_dispatch`) once. Actio emits both trigger blocks (the `workflow_dispatch` inputs derive from the shared inputs) and normalizes every `${{ github.event.inputs.x }}` reference to the canonical `${{ inputs.x }}`. Stripped from the generated workflow YAML.
reusable.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: one declaration, both triggers.

Declare shared inputs once; Actio emits `on.workflow_call` plus `on.workflow_dispatch` (its inputs derived from yours) and rewrites the dispatch-only footgun `${{ github.event.inputs.x }}` (null under `workflow_call`) to the canonical `${{ inputs.x }}` everywhere. Set `dispatch: false` to emit only `workflow_call`.

```yaml
reusable:
  inputs:
    target: { type: string, required: true }
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo "to ${{ inputs.target }}"
```

inject.title: inject (Actio macro)
inject.description: Actio macro. Splice steps here. Local form `inject: name` splices a top-level `fragments:` entry; cross-file form `inject: ./lib.actio.yml#name` imports a step/fragment/template from another file (pair with `with:` to bind its params). Replaced inline by `actio build`.
inject.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: splice steps here.

- **Local**: `inject: setup` splices the named entry from top-level `fragments:`.
- **Cross-file**: `inject: ./lib.actio.yml#name` imports a step, fragment, or template define from another file (relative path, `.actio.yml`/`.yaml`, `#name` required, no `@ref`). Add `with:` to bind the define's `params`.

Replaced inline by `actio build`; nothing survives into the generated workflow.

```yaml
steps:
  - inject: setup
  - inject: ./lib.actio.yml#setupNode
    with: { node: 20 }
  - run: npm test
```

static-if.title: static-if (Actio macro)
static-if.description: Actio macro. Compile-time structural condition. If false, this entire step map is omitted from emitted workflow YAML.
static-if.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: compile-time structural condition.

Evaluated at transpile time against compile-time values (`params.*`, active `for-each` bindings like `<var>`/`for-each.<var>`/`key`/`index`, or custom compile-time symbols). If the expression is false the entire step is removed from the generated workflow; if true the `static-if` key is stripped and the rest is emitted verbatim. Boolean literals are accepted for passes that pre-resolve the condition. Use `if:` for runtime conditions.

```yaml
steps:
  - name: Publish preview
    static-if: params.deploy
    run: ./publish.sh
```

unsafe.description: Actio macro (injection-hoist). Opt this step out of injection-hoist entirely (`${{ }}` left inline in `run:`). Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

trust.description: Actio macro (injection-hoist). Context paths that should NOT be hoisted out of `run:` even if untrusted (you vouch for them). Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

force.description: Actio macro (injection-hoist). Context paths to hoist into `env:` even if classified trusted. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

ensure.title: ensure (Actio macro)
ensure.description: Actio macro. Same-step teardown. The listed steps are spliced in right after this step, each guarded with `if: always()`, so they run even if the step fails or is cancelled.

on-success.title: on-success (Actio macro)
on-success.description: Actio macro. Steps to run after this step only when the step succeeded. Guarded with `if: success() && steps.<id>.outcome == 'success'`.

on-failure.title: on-failure (Actio macro)
on-failure.description: Actio macro. Steps to run after this step only when the step failed. Guarded with `if: !cancelled() && steps.<id>.outcome == 'failure'` (keys on outcome, pre-continue-on-error).

on-abort.title: on-abort (Actio macro)
on-abort.description: Actio macro. Steps to run after this step on cancellation. At step scope this only sees step-level cancellation; run-level cancel belongs in a workflow `finally:`.

^static-if\\(.+\\)$.description: Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.

static-if.title: static-if (Actio macro)
static-if.description: Actio macro. Compile-time structural condition. If false, this entire job map is omitted from emitted workflow YAML.
static-if.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: compile-time structural condition.

Evaluated at transpile time against compile-time values (`params.*`, active `for-each` bindings like `<var>`/`for-each.<var>`/`key`/`index`, or custom compile-time symbols). If the expression is false the entire job is removed from the generated workflow; if true the `static-if` key is stripped and the rest is emitted verbatim. Boolean literals are accepted for passes that pre-resolve the condition. Use `if:` for runtime conditions.

```yaml
jobs:
  release:
    static-if: params.deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./release.sh
```

ensure.title: ensure (Actio macro)
ensure.description: Actio macro. Same-job teardown. The listed steps are appended to this job, each guarded with `if: always()`, so they run even if earlier steps fail or the job is cancelled (bounded by the job timeout).

on-success.title: on-success (Actio macro)
on-success.description: Actio macro. Steps appended to this job that run only when the job succeeded. Guarded with `if: success()`.

on-failure.title: on-failure (Actio macro)
on-failure.description: Actio macro. Steps appended to this job that run only when the job failed. Guarded with `if: failure()`.

on-abort.title: on-abort (Actio macro)
on-abort.description: Actio macro. Steps appended to this job that run only when the job was cancelled. Guarded with `if: cancelled()` (bounded by the job timeout).

executor.description: Actio macro. Name (or ordered list of names) from top-level `executors:`. Expanded during compile and stripped from output.

^static-if\\(.+\\)$.description: Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.
Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.

ifChanged.title: if-changed (Actio macro)
ifChanged.description: Actio macro. Gate this step or job on file changes. Runs only when files changed in the pull request or push match one of these glob patterns. Compiles to a single shared `dorny/paths-filter` setup job plus a `needs.<setup>.outputs.<flag> == 'true'` guard folded into the existing `if:`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.
ifChanged.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: file-change gating.

Runs this step or job only when files changed in the pull request or push match one of the glob patterns. Actio synthesizes one shared setup job that runs [`dorny/paths-filter`](https://github.com/dorny/paths-filter) once, publishes a flag output per unique glob group, and folds `if: needs.<setup>.outputs.<flag> == 'true'` (AND-combined with any existing `if:`) plus `needs:` into each guarded node.

```yaml
steps:
  - name: Build docs
    run: ./build-docs.sh
    if-changed:
      - 'docs/**'
      - '*.md'
```

forEach.title: for-each (Actio macro)
forEach.description: Actio macro. Repeat a step body or fan out a job over compile-time values, runtime expressions, or generated JSON. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

var.description: Compile-time loop variable name available as `{{ <var> }}` in the loop body.

in.description: Loop source: a literal list, object generator (`run`/`script`), compile-time expression, or runtime `${{ ... }}` expression.

parallel.description: At job scope, fan out with a matrix by default; set false for serial job expansion. Step-scope loops must be serial.

as.description: Matrix axis or output alias for the loop value.

key.description: Object field to use when deriving stable serial job IDs.

fail-fast.description: Matrix fail-fast setting for job-scope loops.

expandMatrix.title: expand_matrix (Actio macro)
expandMatrix.description: Actio macro. Unroll a compile-time `strategy.matrix` into one discrete named job per leg, so downstream jobs can `needs:` a single leg via the selector syntax `job(axis=value)`. Requires a fully literal matrix (no `${{ }}`); stripped from generated workflow YAML.
expandMatrix.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: unroll a compile-time matrix into named jobs.

Set `expand_matrix: true` on a job with a literal `strategy.matrix`. Actio emits one job per leg with a deterministic slug (`build-linux-x64`), rewrites every `${{ matrix.* }}` reference to the concrete leg value, and lets other jobs target a leg with the `needs:` selector `job(axis=value)`.

```yaml
jobs:
  build:
    expand_matrix: true
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [linux, windows]
        arch: [x64, arm64]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - run: ./build --arch ${{ matrix.arch }}
  deploy:
    needs: build(os=linux, arch=x64)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./deploy.sh
```

The matrix must be compile-time known; runtime matrices (`${{ fromJSON(...) }}`) stay native. GitHub caps a matrix at 256 jobs, so >256 legs is a build error.

anyOf.description: Actio macro. An expand_matrix leg selector `job(axis=value, ...)`. A partial selector (subset of axes) targets every matching leg. Rewritten to concrete job slug(s) at compile time.

actioJobNeeds.description: Identifies jobs that must complete before this job runs. A string or array of strings. Actio additionally accepts an expand_matrix leg selector `job(axis=value)` that is rewritten to concrete job slug(s) at compile time.

callTemplateDefinition.description: Actio macro. A named reusable-workflow call-job template referenced by `jobs.<id>.extends`. May carry only call-job plumbing keys.

extendsRef.description: Actio macro. The name (or ordered list of names) of one or more `call-templates:` entries a call job inherits via `extends:`. Later templates win; the job's own inline keys win over all.

extendsCallJob.description: Actio macro. A reusable-workflow call job whose `uses` is supplied by a `call-templates:` entry via `extends:`. Inline keys override the template.

importJob.title: inject (Actio cross-file import)
importJob.description: Actio macro. Import a job define from another `.actio.yml` file. `inject` is a `./path.actio.yml#name` selector; `with` binds the define's `params`; any sibling keys deep-merge over the imported job (maps merge recursively, arrays replace). The local job key becomes the emitted job id. Resolved and inlined by `actio build`; nothing survives into the generated workflow.
importJob.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: import a job from another `.actio.yml` file.

`inject` is a `./lib.actio.yml#name` selector (relative path, `.actio.yml`/`.yaml`, `#name` required, no `@ref`). `with` binds the imported define's `params`; sibling keys deep-merge over the imported job (maps merge, arrays replace). The **local job key** becomes the emitted job id.

```yaml
jobs:
  deploy:
    inject: ./lib.actio.yml#deployJob
    with: { env: prod }
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
```

inject.title: inject (Actio cross-file import)
inject.description: Cross-file selector `./lib.actio.yml#jobName`: relative path (`./` or `../`), `.actio.yml`/`.yaml` extension, `#name` required, no `@ref`.

with.description: Actio macro. Compile-time argument map binding the imported define's `params`. The only importer-to-define channel; all other `{{ }}` in the define resolve in its own source file's scope.

share.title: share (Actio macro)
share.description: Actio macro. Publish step outputs so other jobs can consume them. Each entry maps an output `<name>` to a scalar value (value-form) or a captured command (`run`, capture-form). Reference anywhere with `${{ share.<name> }}`; Actio wires `job.outputs` and `needs` automatically and strips the `share` key from the generated workflow.
share.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: publish step outputs cross-job.

Each entry maps an output `<name>` to a value. Reference it anywhere with `${{ share.<name> }}` and Actio auto-wires `job.outputs` + `needs`.

- **value-form** — scalar shorthand or `{ value, required?, type? }`.
- **capture-form** — `{ run, json?, type? }` captures command stdout via a heredoc.
- **json: true** lets consumers read fields: `${{ share.<name>.<field> }}` lowers to `fromJSON(...)`.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: VERSION=$(jq -r .version package.json)
    share:
      version: $VERSION
```

refProducer.title: ref (Actio macro)
refProducer.description: Actio macro. Mark this step as a named reference producer so other steps and jobs can read its outputs by a rename-safe handle with `${{ ref.<handle>.<output> }}`. Actio assigns the step id, synthesizes `job.outputs`, and wires `needs` automatically, then strips the `ref` key from the generated workflow. Non-minted producers (an action `uses:` or a hand-written `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` `run:`) must declare their `outputs:` explicitly.
refProducer.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: expose this step's outputs by a logical handle.

Reference the outputs anywhere with `${{ ref.<handle>.<output> }}` (qualify cross-job collisions as `${{ ref.<job>.<handle>.<output> }}`). Actio assigns the step id, synthesizes `jobs.<id>.outputs`, and adds `needs` edges, all rename-safe. A dotted `${{ ref.<handle>.<output>.<field> }}` lowers to `fromJSON(...)`.

```yaml
steps:
  - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    ref:
      handle: node
      outputs: [node-version]
```

handle.description: Explicit logical handle for this producer. Defaults to a slug of the step `name`, then the step id. A handle that collides with another producer in the same job is a `ref-ambiguous` compile error.

outputs.description: The output names this step publishes. Required for non-minted producers (G3): action `uses:` outputs and hand-written `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` writes are not sniffed.

sharedOutput.title: share.<name> (Actio macro)
sharedOutput.description: A shared output definition: a scalar shorthand, a value-form object (`value`), or a capture-form object (`run`).

oneOf.description: Scalar shorthand value-form: the shell value to publish (e.g. `$VERSION`).

value.description: The shell value to publish as this output.

required.description: Opt-in runtime guard. When true, Actio appends a non-empty assertion that fails the step with `::error::empty share value` if the value is blank.

json.description: Declare the value as JSON so consumers can read fields via `${{ share.<name>.<field> }}` (lowered to `fromJSON(...)`).

run.description: Capture-form: command whose stdout is captured into this output via a heredoc (`ACTIO_EOF_<name>`).

json.description: Declare the captured value as JSON so consumers can read fields via `${{ share.<name>.<field> }}` (lowered to `fromJSON(...)`).

required.description: Opt-in runtime guard for empty captured output.

sharedOutputType.description: Compile-time type assertion for the shared output. Boolean is a type assertion only — Actio does NOT coerce the runtime value.

actioTemplate.title: templates.<name> (Actio macro)
actioTemplate.description: A parameterized, named step list. `params:` declares typed compile-time inputs; `steps:` is the body spliced at the inject site.

params.description: Typed compile-time parameters, referenced in the body with `{{ args.<name> }}`.

steps.description: The ordered list of steps to inject wherever this template is referenced.

default.description: JSON-like default value. Use `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` for string interpolation.

jobExecutionShape.description: Normal jobs need `runs-on` or Actio `executor`; call jobs need `uses` or Actio `extends`. Top-level `job-defaults.runs-on` can satisfy normal jobs globally.

retry.title: retry (Actio macro)
retry.description: Actio macro. Retry a flaky step with optional backoff; expands into a chain of conditional attempts where attempt N runs only if attempt N-1 failed. Shorthand `retry: 3` is the same as `retry: { attempts: 3 }`.
retry.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: retry a flaky step.

Expands into a chain of conditional attempts: attempt _N_ runs only if attempt _N-1_ failed. Shorthand `retry: 3` is the same as `retry: { attempts: 3 }`.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: flaky-command
    retry:
      attempts: 3
      delay: 10s
```

delay.description: Backoff between attempts: a duration like "10s", "2m", "1h", or a number of seconds. Injects `sleep` steps between attempts.

artifacts.title: artifacts (Actio macro)
artifacts.description: Actio macro. Upload build output from this step. Expands into a trailing `actions/upload-artifact` step right after this one, mapping `paths`->`path`, `name`->`name`, and `retention-days`. When `name` is omitted a deterministic, collision-free name is derived (so two uploads never crash upload-artifact@v4 at runtime). The upload step defaults to `if: always()` so artifacts publish even when the step failed.
artifacts.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: upload build output from this step.

Expands into a trailing `actions/upload-artifact` step right after this one. `paths` (a glob string or list of globs) maps to the action's multiline `path` input. `name` and `retention-days` are optional; when `name` is omitted Actio derives a deterministic, collision-free name so two uploads never share the default name (which makes upload-artifact@v4 hard-fail at runtime). The upload defaults to `if: always()` so artifacts publish even on failure (handy for debugging).

```yaml
steps:
  - run: npm run build
    artifacts:
      paths: dist/**
      name: build-output
      retention-days: 7
```

paths.title: paths
paths.description: A glob string or a list of globs to upload. Maps to upload-artifact's `path` input (a list joins into the multiline form).

name.description: Artifact name. Omit to let Actio derive a unique, deterministic name from the job id and step.

if.description: Runtime condition for the upload step. Default `always()` so artifacts publish even when the step failed.

retention-days.description: Days to retain the artifact (GitHub allows 1-90).

softFail.title: soft_fail (Actio macro)
softFail.description: Actio macro. Let a step tolerate failure without failing the job. `true` tolerates any non-zero exit (compiles to `continue-on-error: true`). A list of exit codes (Buildkite-style) compiles a `run` step into a build-time shell wrapper that maps only those codes to success and re-exits any other code. Lists apply to `run` steps on bash, sh, or pwsh.
softFail.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: tolerate specific failures without failing the job.

- `soft_fail: true` tolerates any non-zero exit. Compiles to `continue-on-error: true`, so the step still shows as failed but the job continues. Works on `run` and `uses` steps.
- `soft_fail: [0, 42]` tolerates only the listed exit codes. Compiles a `run` step into a build-time wrapper that exits 0 for a listed code (the step shows green) and re-exits the real code otherwise. Supported on bash, sh, and pwsh.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: ./flaky-check.sh
    soft_fail: [0, 42]
```

oneOf.description: Exit codes to tolerate. Only these codes map to success.

fallback.title: fallback (Actio macro)
fallback.description: Actio macro. A native try/catch. Default (notify): the fallback runs on failure via `if: failure()` but the job still fails. With `recover: true` the guarded step gets `continue-on-error: true` and the fallback keys off `outcome`, so the job can pass.
fallback.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: a native try/catch.

- **notify** (default): the fallback runs on failure via `if: failure()`, but the job still fails.
- **recover** (`recover: true`): the guarded step gets `continue-on-error: true` and the fallback keys off `outcome`, so the job can pass.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: deploy
    fallback:
      recover: true
      steps:
        - run: ./rollback.sh
```

oneOf.description: Fallback steps (notify mode). The job still fails.

steps.description: Steps to run when the guarded step or job fails.

recover.description: When true, swallow the guarded failure (continue-on-error) so the job can continue. Step-level only; ignored on job-level fallback.

retry.description: Re-run the guarded step in a sibling job on a different runner when it fails. Step-level only.

runs-on.description: Runner for the retry job (label, list of labels, or runner mapping).

when-exit-code.description: Only retry when the original step exits with one of these codes. Run-only and requires a POSIX shell (bash/sh).

dynamicMatrix.title: dynamic-matrix (Actio macro)
dynamicMatrix.description: Actio macro. Build a matrix at runtime: `script` prints a JSON array (or `{include:[...]}`) to stdout. Actio generates a setup job that publishes the JSON and wires this job to consume it via `fromJSON()` with a fail-fast:false default and an empty-matrix guard.
dynamicMatrix.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: build a matrix at runtime.

`script` prints a JSON array (or `{ include: [...] }`) to stdout. Actio generates a setup job that publishes the JSON and wires this job to consume it via `fromJSON()`, with `fail-fast: false` and an empty-matrix guard by default.

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    dynamic-matrix:
      script: ./list-targets.sh
      alias: target
    steps:
      - run: ./test ${{ matrix.target }}
```

script.description: Command or local script path that prints a JSON array (or `{"include":[...]}`) to stdout.

alias.description: Wrap a scalar JSON array under `matrix.<alias>` (referenced as `${{ matrix.<alias> }}`). Omit for raw `{include:[...]}` mode.

mode.description: Select fan-out shape explicitly instead of inferring from `alias`. `include`: the script's array (or `{include:[...]}`) passes straight into `strategy.matrix` for heterogeneous per-item `runs-on`/`env`/etc. `alias`: wrap a scalar array under `matrix.<alias>` (requires `alias`).

runs-on.description: Runner for the generated setup job. Defaults to the job's `runs-on`, else `ubuntu-latest`.

shell.description: Shell that runs `script` in the setup job, like a step's `shell`. Supported: bash, sh, pwsh, powershell, python. Actio emits matching plumbing to publish the matrix output; other shells (e.g. cmd) are rejected.

compact.description: Pipe the script's stdout through `jq -c .` to normalize the matrix JSON to one line. POSIX-only (bash/sh); ignored for pwsh/powershell/python, which emit raw multi-line output that `fromJSON` parses fine. Defaults to true.

checkout.description: Check out the repository in the setup job. Defaults to true when `script` is a local path.

fail-fast.description: Value for `strategy.fail-fast` on the matrix job. Defaults to false.

id.description: Override the generated setup job id.

injectionHoistMode.title: injection-hoist mode (Actio macro)
injectionHoistMode.description: Actio macro. Injection-hoist behavior for `run:` `${{ }}` interpolation: `fix` (hoist untrusted values into env and rewrite the body, default), `warn`, `error`, or `off`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

coercionMode.title: coercion mode (Actio macro)
coercionMode.description: Actio macro. YAML type-coercion guard: `fix` (single-quote emitted string scalars a YAML 1.1 consumer would mis-type, default), `warn` (diagnose, leave unquoted), or `off`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

lifecycleHook.title: lifecycle hook (Actio macro)
lifecycleHook.description: Actio macro. An ordered list of teardown/outcome steps attached to a step or job. Compiled into guarded steps and stripped from the generated workflow.

on-success.description: Actio macro. Named teardown jobs that run only when the workflow succeeded (`if: success()`).

on-failure.description: Actio macro. Named teardown jobs that run only when the workflow failed (`if: failure()`).

on-abort.description: Actio macro. Cancel-path teardown. A mapping of named jobs replaces the auto cancel-companion; an empty list (`[]`) opts out of any cancel job while the normal-path aggregator still runs.

oneOf.description: Explicit opt-out: emit no cancel-path job.

finallyBranchGroup.description: Actio macro. A mapping of named teardown jobs for one outcome branch.

finallyJob.description: Actio macro. A teardown job. Accepts standard job keys plus the Actio `when:` sugar; Actio injects `needs:` over all real jobs and the appropriate outcome guard at compile time.

when.title: when (Actio macro)
when.description: Actio sugar inside a finally job. Gate on another job's result, e.g. `deploy.failed` → `needs.deploy.result == 'failure'`. States: failed, succeeded, cancelled, skipped. The referenced job is force-added to this job's `needs:`.

reusableWorkflow.description: Actio macro. Generates `on.workflow_call` (always) and `on.workflow_dispatch` (unless `dispatch: false`) from one shared declaration, deriving the dispatch inputs from the shared inputs.

inputs.description: Shared input definitions used for both triggers. Types are limited to the workflow_call subset (string, boolean, number) so the same definition is valid for workflow_dispatch.

dispatch.description: Whether to also emit `on.workflow_dispatch`. Defaults to true; set to false to generate a call-only workflow while still normalizing input references.

reusableInput.description: Actio macro. A shared input definition valid for both workflow_call and workflow_dispatch. `type` defaults to string when omitted.

type.description: Input type. Limited to the subset shared by workflow_call and workflow_dispatch.

required.description: Whether the input must be supplied.

default.description: Default value used when the input is omitted.
